DTR  |  Listen to a Classroom Conversation
LCC-HS12-L3-13001400-HSVPAMA1112-SPK-EUR-01 | HS Visual & Performing Arts: Media Arts 11-12 | Consolidating Purpose, Intention, and Style | Spellbook of Knowledge | European Historical & Contemporary Contexts

The Tenth Vow

Click  🎧  to listen.
Listen to a conversation between a teacher and a girl. Listen carefully. You will hear the conversation only once. After listening, you will answer some questions.
Listen to the Conversation
1QUESTION 1: What are the teacher and the girl mainly talking about?
2QUESTION 2: According to the teacher, why did the Danish directors create the Dogme 95 rules?
3QUESTION 3: What does the girl come to understand by the end of the conversation?
Choose the best answer for each question. You may change your answers before submitting.
Answer all 3 questions to submit.
SMART SUPPORT:
Go back to the conversation. What evidence in the teacher's framing tells you the actual topic of the exchange, and what evidence in the teacher's account explains why the directors created the rules?
CA ELD PI.A.1, PI.A.3, PI.B.5, PI.B.6  |  CCSS.ELA-LITERACY.SL.11-12.3  |  HS Media Arts 11-12: Purpose, Intention, and Style
